page: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4 GB of memory per vCPU. Storage capabilities include a bundled 1229 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU encryption tests ranking in the top 25%. Memory performance is also a highlight, with the PassMark memory score placing in the top tier. Multi-core performance, software compilation, and database operations fall within the average range. For language model workloads, the instance achieves strong text generation speeds on small 135M models, but performance drops significantly on larger models, with prompt processing for 70B models falling into the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32ads_v5 offers a balanced resource density, combining a high-capacity local SSD with a standard 1:4 CPU-to-RAM ratio. This makes it a viable option for workloads requiring local scratch space without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. Recommended workloads include general-purpose applications, web serving, software compilation, database operations, and light machine learning inference on smaller models.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance includes a bundled 1229 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, high encryption throughput, and excellent memory scores, while multi-core processing, database operations, and software compilation remain average. Although highly capable for small-scale language model inference, performance degrades on larger models, such as 70B parameter architectures. The D32ads_v5 represents a balanced resource profile, offering qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require a mix of compute, memory, and high-capacity local storage. It is well-suited for web serving, database management, software development, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 1229 GB local disk, delivering strong single-core and encryption performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ads_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high-speed memory, and a substantial bundled local SSD.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 128 GB RAM and a bundled 1229 GB local SSD
Strong single-core CPU, memory, and encryption benchmark performance
Average multi-core, database, and software compilation capabilities
Suitable for web serving, database operations, and small-scale LLM inference
